verses.text: karmanye vadhikaraste ma phaleshu kadacana
verses.meaning: You have a right to perform your prescribed duties, but you are not entitled to the fruits of your actions. Never consider yourself the cause of the results of your activities, nor be attached to inaction.

verses.text: yogasthah kuru karmani sangam tyaktva dhananjaya
verses.meaning: Perform your duty equipoised, O Arjuna, abandoning all attachment to success or failure. Such equanimity is called yoga.

verses.text: buddhiyukto jahatiha ubhe sukrta duskrte
verses.meaning: Endowed with such a faith, he seeks the favor of a particular demigod and obtains his desires. But in reality these benefits are bestowed by Me alone.

verses.text: tasmad asaktah satatam karyam karma samacara
verses.meaning: Therefore, without attachment, perform your duty, for by working without attachment one attains the Supreme.

verses.text: yada yada hi dharmasya glanir bhavati bharata
verses.meaning: Whenever and wherever there is a decline in religious practice, O descendant of Bharata, and a predominant rise of irreligion — at that time I descend Myself.

verses.text: paritranaya sadhunam vinasaya ca duskrtam
verses.meaning: To deliver the pious and to annihilate the miscreants, as well as to reestablish the principles of religion, I Myself appear, millennium after millennium.

verses.text: bhoktaram yajnatapasam sarvaloka mahesvaram
verses.meaning: The sages, knowing Me as the ultimate purpose of all sacrifices and austerities, the Supreme Lord of all worlds, and the friend of all living entities, attain peace.

verses.text: uddhared atmanatmanam natmanam avasadayet
verses.meaning: One must deliver himself with the help of his mind, and not degrade himself. The mind is the friend of the conditioned soul, and his enemy as well.

verses.text: bandhur atmatmanas tasya yenatmaivatmana jitaḥ
verses.meaning: For him who has conquered the mind, the mind is the best of friends; but for one who has failed to do so, his mind will remain the greatest enemy.

verses.text: asamsayam maha-baho mano durnigraham calam
verses.meaning: O mighty-armed son of Kunti, it is undoubtedly very difficult to curb the restless mind, but it is possible by suitable practice and by detachment.

verses.text: mattah parataram nanyat kincid asti dhananjaya
verses.meaning: O conqueror of wealth, there is no truth superior to Me. Everything rests upon Me, as pearls are strung on a thread.

verses.text: bahunam janmanam ante jnanavan mam prapadyate
verses.meaning: After many births and deaths, he who is actually in knowledge surrenders unto Me, knowing Me to be the cause of all causes and all that is.

verses.text: ananyas cintayanto mam ye janah paryupasate
verses.meaning: But those who always worship Me with exclusive devotion, meditating on My transcendental form — to them I carry what they lack, and I preserve what they have.

verses.text: yat karosi yad asnasi yaj juhosi dadasi yat
verses.meaning: Whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer or give away, and whatever austerities you perform — do that, O son of Kunti, as an offering to Me.

verses.text: man-mana bhava mad-bhakto mad-yaji mam namaskuru
verses.meaning: Engage your mind always in thinking of Me, become My devotee, offer obeisances to Me, and worship Me. Being completely absorbed in Me, surely you will come to Me.

verses.text: aham sarvasya prabhavo mattah sarvam pravartate
verses.meaning: I am the source of all spiritual and material worlds. Everything emanates from Me. The wise who perfectly know this engage in My devotional service.

verses.text: advesta sarva-bhutanam maitrah karuna eva ca
verses.meaning: One who is not envious but is a kind friend to all living entities, who does not think himself a proprietor and is free from false ego, who is equal in both happiness and distress...

verses.text: ye tu dharmamrtam idam yathoktam paryupasate
verses.meaning: Those who follow this imperishable path of devotional service and who completely engage themselves with faith, making Me the supreme goal, are very, very dear to Me.

verses.text: amanitvam adambhitvam ahimsa ksantir arjavam
verses.meaning: Humility; pridelessness; nonviolence; tolerance; simplicity; approaching a bona fide spiritual master; cleanliness; steadiness; self-control...

verses.text: mamaivamso jiva-loke jiva-bhutah sanatanah
verses.meaning: The living entities in this conditioned world are My eternal, fragmental parts. Due to the conditioned life, they are struggling very hard with the six senses, which include the mind.

verses.text: trividham narakasyedam dvaram nasanam atmanah
verses.meaning: There are three gates leading to this hell — lust, anger and greed. Every sane man should give these up, for they lead to the degradation of the soul.

verses.text: sattvanurupa sarvasya sraddha bhavati bharata
verses.meaning: O son of Bharata, according to one's existence under the various modes of nature, one evolves a particular kind of faith. The living being is said to be of a particular faith according to the modes he has acquired.

verses.text: man-mana bhava mad-bhakto mad-yaji mam namaskuru
verses.meaning: Always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your homage unto Me. Thus you will come to Me without fail. I promise you this because you are My very dear friend.

verses.text: sarva-dharman parityajya mam ekam saranam vraja
verses.meaning: Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.

verses.text: sri-bhagavan uvaca asocyan anvasocas tvam prajna-vadams ca bhasase
verses.meaning: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: While speaking learned words, you are mourning for what is not worthy of grief. Those who are wise lament neither for the living nor for the dead.

verses.text: dehino smin yatha dehe kaumaram yauvanam jara
verses.meaning: As the embodied soul continuously passes, in this body, from boyhood to youth to old age, the soul similarly passes into another body at death. A sober person is not bewildered by such a change.

verses.text: matra-sparas tu kaunteya sitosna-sukha-duhkha-dah
verses.meaning: O son of Kunti, the nonpermanent appearance of happiness and distress, and their disappearance in due course, are like the appearance and disappearance of winter and summer seasons. They arise from sense perception, O scion of Bharata, and one must learn to tolerate them without being disturbed.

verses.text: yam hi na vyathayanty ete purusam purusarsabha
verses.meaning: O best among men [Arjuna], the person who is not disturbed by happiness and distress and is steady in both is certainly eligible for liberation.

verses.text: na jayate mriyate va kadacin nayam bhutva bhavita va na bhuyah
verses.meaning: For the soul there is neither birth nor death at any time. He has not come into being, does not come into being, and will not come into being. He is unborn, eternal, ever-existing and primeval. He is not slain when the body is slain.

verses.text: vasamsi jirnani yatha vihaya navani grhnati naro parani
verses.meaning: As a person puts on new garments, giving up old ones, the soul similarly accepts new material bodies, giving up the old and useless ones.
